design_philosophy.core_loop: Select wrestler → engage in ring combat with unique moves → pin opponent for three-count → progress through increasingly difficult opponents → defend championship title
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time wrestling match execution: timing lock-ups, managing positioning relative to referee, executing specials vs. opponent's defense
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a professional wrestler, master your character's signature moves, climb to championship, defend title against all comers
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First wrestling game to authentically simulate wrestling mechanics (referee positioning affects pin timing, 20-count ring exit rule, lock-up system for special moves). Designed by Masato Masuda as a core simulation of professional wrestling rather than a fighting game with wrestling flavor. Emphasizes positioning and timing over button mashing.

difficulty.checkpointing: Championship ladder: win 5 matches to challenge VWA Champion, then defend title 10 times (2 against each opponent) to face Great Puma final boss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides asymmetric difficulty (King Slender notably harder due to bug requiring extra wins). Two-player mode removes single-player difficulty curve. Best-of-three format in multiplayer vs. single-pinfall in single-player

progression.unlock_mechanism: Championship ladder progression: unlock title shot after 5 wins → unlock Great Puma fight after 10 title defenses → inter-promotional championship conclusion
replayability_hooks: Six distinct wrestlers with unique movesets encourage multiple playthroughs
Two-player competitive mode with best-of-three format
Asymmetric difficulty per character (King Slender has documented bug)
Referee positioning creates dynamic match outcomes
Ring exit mechanics and count-out strategy layer

world_building_techniques: Wrestler names and nationalities (Fighter Hayabusa/Japan, Giant Panther/USA, Kin Corn Karn/Korea, King Slender/USA, Starman/Mexico, The Amazon/Parts Unknown)
Championship structure (VWA Champion → VWF Champion → Inter-promotional title)
Ringside commentators and cameraman establish sports broadcast authenticity
Regional name changes (FWA to VWA, championship logos) suggest fictional wrestling federation

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss (Great Puma) represents ultimate challenge after title defense gauntlet. Positioned as superior federation champion (VWF), creating inter-promotional narrative. Described by Nintendo aficionados as among most difficult NES boss characters.
notable_bosses: King Slender - VWA Champion encountered after 5 wins; acts as mid-game gatekeeper to championship phase
Great Puma - VWF Champion; final encounter after 10 title defenses; considered one of hardest NES boss characters

faction_indicators: VWA (Video Wrestling Association) vs VWF (Video Wrestling Federation) inter-promotional divide
Regional wrestler identities suggest distinct wrestling traditions (Japanese submission style vs American power style implied)
danger_signals: Great Puma final boss difficulty spike
King Slender bug creating unfair progression for that character
20-count ring exit rule creates edge-case failures for skilled players
environmental_storytelling: Ring logo changes (FWA→VWA) signal federation branding
Referee presence and positioning creates authentic sports broadcast feel
Character portraits on select screen establish individual wrestler identity
Championship belt defense structure mimics real professional wrestling hierarchy

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Pro Wrestling (NES, Famicom Disk System)
